data.title: Biggest stock movers Thursday: PRAX, HPE, GRAL, FOSL, VERI, and more
data.description:
Stock index futures advanced on Thursday as investors awaited a few speeches from the Federal Reserve members.

Here are some of Thursday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Praxis Precision Medicines (NASDAQ:PRAX) +196.44% - Stock jumped dramatically after announcing positive topline results from its Phase 3 Essential3 program for ulixacaltamide in essential tremor, the commonest type of movement disorder. The drug successfully met the primary endpoint in the first study (Study 1), demonstrating a statistically significant improvement in the mADL11 score at Week 8. This success in both key Phase 3 trials drove the stock's massive gain. The company expects to submit a new drug application to the FDA in early 2026 to secure marketing approval for ulixacaltamide and request a pre-NDA meeting ahead of the filing.
  • J.B. Hunt Transport Services (NASDAQ:JBHT) +17.69% - Shares surged after the company topped Q3 estimates. Revenue fell 0.7% Y/Y to $3.05B, beating expectations by $30M, while EPS rose 18% to $1.76 vs. the $1.46 consensus. Operating income increased 8% to $242.7M, led by stronger productivity in the Dedicated Contract Services segment with higher revenue per truck and improved weekly performance. Management cited cost efficiencies, operational improvements, and confidence in its long-term strategy focused on safety and value delivery.

  • GRAIL (NASDAQ: NASDAQ:GRAL) +15% - Shares surged after an announcement that Samsung Electronics, Samsung C&T, and GRAIL (NASDAQ: GRAL) have signed a binding letter of intent to collaborate on expanding the availability of GRAIL's Galleri multi-cancer early detection test in key Asian markets. As part of the deal, Samsung will invest $110 million in GRAIL at a price of $70.05 per share. The commercialization efforts will be led by Samsung C&T in South Korea, with plans to potentially expand into Japan and Singapore thereafter.

Biggest stock losers

  • Shares of U.S. watchmaker Fossil (NASDAQ: NASDAQ:FOSL) -25.1% - Shares decline sharply after following the company's announcement that it has once again extended the deadline for the exchange offer related to its 7% senior notes due 2026. Fossil, currently undergoing debt restructuring, has pushed the deadline back by one week to October 22. This marks the second time the company has delayed the exchange process, which was originally announced in September.

  • Veritone (NASDAQ: NASDAQ:VERI) -20.3% - Shares declined following the news of the capital raise and resulting dilution. It announced a $75 million registered direct offering, selling approximately 12.86 million shares at a price of $5.83 per share. The deal, managed by Needham & Company, is expected to close around October 17, 2025, subject to standard closing conditions. Veritone plans to allocate the proceeds primarily toward working capital, paying down debt, and general corporate purposes.

  • Hewlett Packard Enterprise (NYSE:HPE) -9% - Shares declined after the company’s Securities Analyst Meeting, where FY2026 guidance came in below expectations. HPE forecasts revenue growth of 5–10% vs. a consensus of ~17% and adjusted EPS of $2.20–$2.40 (midpoint $2.30) vs. estimates of $2.42. Longer-term, HPE projected a 5–7% compound annual revenue growth rate and 11–17% non-GAAP operating profit growth through FY2028, targeting EPS of at least $3. The company also announced a 10% dividend hike and a $3B share repurchase increase.

data.title: Don't cry for Ferrari after its 20% drop; UBS calls its strategy a long-time winner
data.description:
Ferrari N.V. (NYSE:RACE) has peeled off close to 20% after the luxury vehicle maker's long-term financial guidance came in significantly below investor expectations. During Ferrari's recent Capital Markets Day, management projected 2030 revenue to reach about €9 billion, which was well short of the roughly €10 billion anticipated by analysts.

UBS analyst Zuzanna Pusz said the investor debate on Ferrari (NYSE:RACE) has shifted from the typical conservative guidance to a fundamental change in the equity story. "However, we believe that committed to its scarcity-driven strategy and respect for its customers, the company has taken the right decision to protect the longevity of its growth by steering clear of the pricing missteps seen across the luxury industry," advised Pusz.

Ferrari (RACE) is seen as taking the correct step to focus on broadening its reach among high net worth individuals. That strategy was reflected in new launches likely to be offered at a lower premium to other models to preserve the perceived value for money, and the first electric Ferrari to be a Range model to recruit from a much broader customer base.

Pusz and the rest of the UBS analyst team continue to see RACE as one of the most compelling long-term growth stories in the sector, even if it may lack positive catalysts in the short term. UBS reiterated its Buy rating on Ferrari (RACE) and set a lowered price target of $529.

Shares of Ferrari (RACE) were up 1.8% in Thursday morning trading.

data.title: Morgan Stanley initiates coverage of Equinix with Overweight, Digital Realty Trust with Equal-weight
data.description:
Morgan Stanley initiated coverage of the data center operator Equinix (NASDAQ:EQIX) with an Overweight rating and Digital Realty Trust (NYSE:DLR) with an Equal-weight rating.

For Equinix, analysts Cameron McVeigh and Benjamin Swinburne said in a research note, "We see opportunity for revenue and AFFO/share growth to accelerate from '26E to '29E as capital investment steps up to service growing industry demand."

"We see Equinix's recently announced long-term guidance as achievable and see an opportunity for multiple re-rating as the market gains confidence. In addition, we are bullish on the network effects and stickier tenant base enabled by its uniquely scaled interconnection ecosystem, which helps de-risk the forecast," said the note.

Morgan Stanley's rating on Equinix aligns with the average sell-side analysts and Seeking Alpha authors rating of Buy. SA's Quant Rating system sees the stock as Hold.

"While we see nearly double digit revenue growth and AFFO / share growth ahead at DLR, we see that growth largely reflected in expectations," said the analysts.

"Longer-term, we see risk to shares that a flat or deflationary pricing environment returns if the hyperscaler tenant base is able to again exert leverage over DC (data center) operators," said the research note.

The rating on DLR aligns with the Quant rating of Hold. Meanwhile, SA authors and Wall Street analysts grade the stock as Buy.

data.title: Homebuilder sentiment perks up in October, more than expected
data.description: The NAHB/Wells Fargo Housing Market Index perked up to 37 in October from 32 in September, beating the 33 consensus, according to data released by the National Association of Homebuilders on Thursday.

data.title: Crown rated Outperform in new research at Raymond James; Ball, Ardagh rated Market Perform
data.description:
Raymond James late Wednesday initiated coverage on three of the world’s biggest beverage can producers: Crown Holdings (NYSE:CCK), Ball Corp. (NYSE:BALL) and Ardagh Metal Packaging (NYSE:AMBP). The firm highlighted structural growth and sustainability trends that continue to favor aluminum packaging.

In an Oct. 15 report titled BevCan You Dig It!, analyst Matt Roberts said the three companies collectively control more than 75% of the beverage can market, yet differ sharply in business mix, strategy and corporate culture. Roberts began coverage with an Outperform rating on Crown Holdingsb(NYSE:CCK) and Market Perform ratings on Ball (NYSE:BALL) and Ardagh (NYSE:AMBP), citing Crown’s stronger near-term positioning in North America.

Industry tailwinds: From TikTok trends to sustainability

The report points to multiple forces driving beverage can demand, from viral social media fads to consumer preferences for recyclable materials. Roberts noted that even emerging drink categories such as energy drinks, prebiotic sodas and “dirty sodas” are leaning toward aluminum packaging, while major beverage companies continue shifting beer and soft drink lines from plastic and glass to cans.

That trend, he said, aligns with both legislative sustainability mandates such as the European Union’s Packaging and Packaging Waste Regulation and the long-term environmental goals of global consumer brands. Unlike some fleeting eco fads, the aluminum can’s sustainability advantage has real momentum, according to Raymond James.

Tight supply, capital discipline support pricing

Raymond James expects the supply-demand balance to remain favorable through 2027, with U.S. can capacity growth slowing to 0% to 1% annually after a major expansion cycle between 2021 and 2023. With expected market growth of around 2%, utilization rates should stay above 90%, helping preserve pricing and margins.

The firm also noted a shift in corporate priorities: Ball (BALL) and Crown (CCK) are focusing on shareholder returns, while Ardagh (AMBP) is working to reduce debt.

“Capital priorities appear to have shifted,” Roberts said “As such, we expect a lid on supply for years to come.”

Short-term headwinds, longer-term opportunity

Despite a supportive long-term outlook, Raymond James cautioned that near-term volume weakness persists across key regions. Soft demand in the U.S. and Europe and slower alcohol output in Brazil are weighing on results, though Roberts said valuations already reflect much of that pressure.

He said he expects Crown Holdings (CCK) to show the most durable growth into 2026, with improving momentum in North America. Ball (BALL) could benefit from stabilization in its U.S. beer business and continued strength in South America, while Ardagh (AMBP) faces a tougher path amid balance-sheet repair efforts.

Roberts said the beverage can sector remains one of the few areas in packaging with sustainable volume growth and rational supply discipline, setting it apart from the broader industrial landscape.

data.title: Li Auto expands Hong Kong presence
data.description:
• As part of a broader effort to expand its international presence and compete more effectively with Chinese rivals, Li Auto (NASDAQ:LI) is leveraging its new Hong Kong headquarters as a "strategic base” according to InvestHK 25.
  • "As the international financial center and I&T hub, Hong Kong provides us with unparalleled resources and a platform to help Li Auto achieve greater breakthroughs on the global stage,” said Li Auto’s financial compliance manager Wang Teenqi.
  • The company’s Hong Kong hub will oversee several functions including overseas investment, financing, global R&D collaboration, and its international supply chain.

data.title: DoorDash and Waymo testing autonomous delivery in Metro Phoenix
data.description:
DoorDash (NASDAQ:DASH) customers in Metro Phoenix can now get deliveries through a Waymo (NASDAQ:GOOG) autonomous vehicle thanks to a new partnership between the two companies.

Testing of the new delivery method is currently underway in the Metro Phoenix area with plans to launch broader commercial operations later this year. By deploying more autonomous delivery methods, DoorDash (NASDAQ:DASH) aims to lower its reliance on drivers and lower costs to compete more aggressively with Uber Eats (UBER), currently running a close second to DoorDash (NASDAQ:DASH) in the food delivery business.

Using DoorDash’s (DASH) autonomous delivery platform, customers can receive delivery from DoorDash’s (DASH) DashMart convenience, grocery and retail stores through either a Dasher, drone, robot, or one of Waymo’s autonomous Jaguars. Once the delivery arrives, customers can unlock the trunk to retrieve their merchandise through the DoorDash app.

While the option to have an AV make deliveries is a more cost-effective option for DoorDash (DASH), how well customers and merchants will embrace the new technology is less certain. For merchants, the order must now be placed in the vehicle rather than handed to a Dasher, and for customers, their order must now be retrieved from the curb rather than have it delivered right to their door. Small inconveniences, but ones that could deter either -- or both -- from using the AV option over more traditional mothods.

Nonetheless, the news pumped up shares of DoorDash (DASH), currently up more than 3% while rival Uber (UBER) is trading with a loss of 0.5%.

data.title: Sea Limited rallies after BofA says growth drivers are fully in play
data.description:
Sea Limited (NYSE:SE) rallied in early trading on Thursday after Bank of America fired off a Buy rating on the digital entertainment stock. 

The firm sees Sea Limited (NYSE:SE) as the best-positioned name in its coverage to ride the e-commerce boom in Southeast Asia due to its increasingly dominant online marketplace (Shopee). In addition, the expansion into Brazil/Latin America was noted to offer further substantial growth upside potential in the long term. 

"Free Fire likely to show steady growth led by expansion in emerging markets and futurepotential collaborations. In Fintech, Sea is expanding offering from on-Shopee to off-Shopee & secured loans," wrote analyst Sachin Salgaonkar on the growth levers in play.

Salgaonkar highlighted that Sea (SEA) stock has largely been range-bound for the last couple of months despite improving momentum on the ground. BofA increased its price objective on SEA to $215 from $206, with an eye on the strong fundamentals across the business. 

Shares of Sea Limited (SE) were up 4.8% in early trading on Thursday to add to the +60% year-to-date run-up. Both Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha analysts have a consensus Buy rating on the stock.

data.title: HPE presents 'prudent' targets as it continues to gain market share: analysts
data.description:
Hewlett Packard Enterprise (NYSE:HPE) outlined a path to grow market share across servers, storage and networking during its Securities Analyst Meeting on Wednesday, but "prudent" growth targets prompted the stock to fall 8% by Thursday morning market action.

Financial firms offered varied reactions to the meeting and outlook.

"Ultimately, HPE focused investors toward a return of capital," said KeyBanc analysts Brandon Nispel and Matt Sbriglio in an investor note. "As we take a step back and compare the strategic focus we saw from Arista Networks (ANET) and Dell's (DELL) recent analyst days, we think ANET has a more defined strategy to grow its networking business in the Data Center, Enterprise, and Campus, and DELL has a more refined strategy to grow its AI Server/Storage business. From our perspective, the HPE narrative was a less convincing ability to execute in both these key AI verticals."

KeyBanc maintained its Sector Weight rating on the stock. They lowered their full-year fiscal 2026 revenue estimate to $40.46B from $41.42B.

Susquehanna reiterated its Neutral rating and $21 price target following the meeting.

"HPE guided FY26 EPS below our estimates, reflecting lower growth and margin dilution from the Juniper acquisition," said Susquehanna analysts, led by Mehdi Hosseini, in a note. "Looking ahead, the company outlined a clear path to generating >$3.00 in EPS by FY28, contingent on its ability to increase market share within the 'Sovereign' Al projects while also expanding Networking OM to 25-28% range as cost synergies materialize. While L-T target is encouraging, we view it as ambitious given the fierce competitive landscape in DC switching and thus maintain Neutral rating."

However, Citi reiterated its Buy rating and $28 price target. They also said the price drop creates a buying opportunity on the stock.

"We believe guide comes from an abundance of prudence, evident in its low-to-mid single-digit outlook for networking (vs industry 9% CAGR)," said Citi analyst Asiya Merchant, in a Thursday note. "Management noted the combined networking business is heavily skewed towards slower-growing  campus/branch, resulting in segment growth likely to remain modest near-term until the gradual shift towards faster-growing areas like DC switching."

Evercore ISI also maintained its Outperform rating and $28 price target.

"HPE plans to take share across servers, storage and networking by leaning into areas where it already has momentum," said Evercore analysts, led by Amit Daryanani. "In networking, HPE +Juniper hold roughly ~18% share in campus and branch, and the company aims to expand globally, particularly outside the U.S. where disruption among Chinese vendors is creating openings. In storage, HPE expects to continue gaining share from a current 10–11% base. In servers, it will prioritize profitable share rather than unit growth through higher average unit pricing and services attach."
